mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency falls below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market slack signals deteriorating consumer demand and corporate earnings pressure, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of monthly observations where UNRATE crosses above the 6.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income growth outpacing consumer price inflation expands real purchasing power, which directly increases corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS growth exceeds CPIAUCSL growth by more than 1.5 percentage points annually.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative 5-day forward returns in greater than 50% of observations during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect rising household confidence and construction sector activity, which correlates with broader economic expansion and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST increases consistently precede negative 60-day S&P 500 returns with a t-statistic below 1.5 across rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 220.0, elevated inflation expectations force monetary authorities to tighten policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of months where CPIAUCSL breaches the 220.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which boosts consumer spending forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal predicts negative returns in greater than 55% of concurrent monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 3.5 percent signals an overheating economy that precedes central bank intervention and market corrections, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 values above 3.5 percent growth consistently coincide with positive 60-day forward returns during late-cycle phases.
